course.name: Test Course - Social Media Basics
course.description: A simple test course to verify the import system works perfectly. Learn the fundamentals of social media marketing for South African small businesses.

modules.title: Module 1: Introduction to Social Media
modules.description: Understand the basics of social media marketing and why it matters for your business.
modules.content:
📱 Introduction to Social Media

Reach Your Customers Where They Spend Their Time

🎯 What You'll Learn

  • ✅ Understand the power of social media for small businesses
  • ✅ Choose the right platforms for your business
  • ✅ Create engaging content that attracts customers
  • ✅ Measure your social media success

Why Social Media Matters

Social media is where your customers spend 2-3 hours every day. By having a strong social media presence, you can reach them where they already are!

💡 Key Insight

You don't need a big budget to succeed on social media. Consistency and authenticity matter more than expensive ads!

The Big Three Platforms for SA Businesses

  • Facebook: 26 million South Africans use it - perfect for reaching adults 25-65
  • Instagram: 10 million SA users - ideal for visual businesses (salons, restaurants, fashion)
  • WhatsApp Business: 90%+ of SA smartphone users - great for direct customer communication

🏪 Real Success Story: Thabo's Bakery - Soweto

Business Type: Small bakery specializing in traditional South African baked goods

Challenge: Foot traffic was slow, only 15 customers per day

Solution: Started posting daily photos of fresh bread and cakes on Facebook and Instagram

📊 Results After 2 Months:

  • ✅ 45 customers per day (3x increase!)
  • ✅ 800 Facebook followers
  • ✅ Pre-orders through WhatsApp every morning

💰 Total Investment: R0 per month (used free organic posts only)

💬 Thabo's Tip: Take photos in good morning light, show your products fresh out of the oven, and respond to every comment within an hour!

📝 Step-by-Step: Create Your First Post

  1. Take a photo: Use your smartphone camera in good lighting
  2. Write a caption: What's special about this product/service?
  3. Add a call-to-action: Visit us today! or Call to order!
  4. Post at peak times: 7-9am, 12-1pm, or 6-8pm
  5. Engage with comments: Reply within 1 hour for best results

💡 Pro Tip: Post consistently! 3 times per week is better than 7 posts one week and nothing the next.

✅ Your Action Plan (Complete This Week)

  1. Day 1: Create business pages on Facebook and Instagram
  2. Day 2: Take 10 photos of your products/services
  3. Day 3: Write 3 post captions and schedule them
  4. Day 4-7: Post once per day and engage with comments

🎯 Key Takeaway

Social media success comes from showing up consistently, being authentic, and engaging with your community - not from having the biggest budget!

questions.question: How many South Africans use Facebook according to the module?
questions.explanation: According to the module, 26 million South Africans use Facebook, making it the largest social media platform in the country and perfect for reaching adults aged 25-65.

questions.question: True or False: You need a big budget to succeed on social media.
questions.explanation: False! The module emphasizes that consistency and authenticity matter more than expensive ads. Thabo's Bakery succeeded with R0 investment using only organic posts.

questions.question: What was Thabo's Bakery's customer increase after 2 months of social media posting?
questions.explanation: Thabo's Bakery went from 15 customers per day to 45 customers per day - a 3x increase! This shows the power of consistent social media posting with quality photos and engagement.

questions.question: According to the module, what percentage of SA smartphone users have WhatsApp?
questions.explanation: Over 90% of South African smartphone users have WhatsApp, making it an essential platform for direct customer communication and order taking.

questions.question: What is the recommended posting frequency according to the Pro Tip?
questions.explanation: The module's Pro Tip states that 3 times per week consistently is better than 7 posts one week and nothing the next. Consistency beats frequency!
